skill.name: api-documentation
skill.description: Create structured API documentation for REST APIs, GraphQL schemas, SDK methods, and developer reference guides.
skill.summary: Create OpenAPI specs, API reference pages, examples, and developer guides for API teams.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, hardcoded URLs, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are documentation examples, OpenAPI fields, placeholder URLs, reference links, and tag text rather than executable code or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Fenced Documentation Examples Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby and shell execution detections occur inside Markdown code fences and API documentation examples. No executable script, tool hook, or runtime command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced YAML, TypeScript, Markdown, JSON, curl examples, a docs tree, or tags. There is no evidence that the skill executes these snippets.

low_findings.title: Example URLs and Reference Links Misidentified as Network Access
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL detections are placeholder API server URLs, curl examples, and public documentation references. The skill does not contain code that initiates network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are inside OpenAPI examples, sample curl commands, and a references section. No fetch, socket, webhook, or exfiltration flow was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak crypto and reconnaissance detections appear to match documentation vocabulary, HTTP descriptions, sample JWT text, and TOON summary fields. No cryptographic API use or system discovery command was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged material is descriptive documentation content and compressed metadata. I found no evidence of hashing, encryption, credential theft, process listing, host probing, or filesystem reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: API docs often drift and miss examples developers need. This skill structures OpenAPI specs, reference pages, examples, and usage guides for API teams.
actual_capabilities: Drafts OpenAPI 3.0 specifications with paths, schemas, responses, and security schemes.
Shows how to annotate Express and TypeScript routes with Swagger documentation blocks.
Outlines Swagger UI setup for interactive API reference pages.
Creates authentication, request, response, error, pagination, and rate limit documentation sections.
Suggests a documentation folder structure for guides, API reference pages, and examples.
limitations: Does not automatically validate generated specs against a running API.
Uses template examples that must be adapted to the real service.
Does not inspect source code unless the user provides the relevant files.
Does not host or deploy Swagger UI or Redoc pages.

use_cases.title: Publish a REST API Reference
use_cases.description: Create OpenAPI paths, request schemas, response schemas, and error documentation for new REST endpoints.

use_cases.title: Prepare Public Developer Docs
use_cases.description: Turn API behavior into clear guides, authentication steps, examples, changelog notes, and reference pages.

use_cases.title: Align Frontend and Backend Teams
use_cases.description: Define endpoint contracts, pagination behavior, rate limits, and error cases before implementation starts.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Endpoint Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Document this REST endpoint for developers. Include purpose, parameters, request body, success response, errors, and one curl example.

prompt_templates.title: Draft an OpenAPI Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an OpenAPI 3.0 specification for these endpoints. Include schemas, security, pagination, and reusable error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing API Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these API docs for missing examples, unclear fields, undocumented errors, authentication gaps, and consistency issues.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Complete Docs Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a complete API documentation set for this service. Include reference pages, getting started guide, auth guide, examples, changelog, and SDK notes.

output_examples.input: A team provides two user endpoints and asks for API reference documentation.
output: Endpoint summaries with method, path, purpose, and authentication requirements.
Parameter, request body, response, and error sections for each endpoint.
Example requests and responses using placeholder data.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for an OpenAPI outline for a new service.
output: A structured specification plan with servers, paths, components, security schemes, and reusable responses.
Schema recommendations for resources, pagination, validation errors, and authentication errors.

output_examples.input: A documentation owner wants a public launch checklist.
output: A checklist covering authentication, examples, SDK links, error handling, rate limits, changelog notes, and review ownership.

best_practices: Provide real endpoint behavior, status codes, and validation rules before asking for final documentation.
Use placeholder credentials and example domains in every generated request sample.
Update docs whenever request fields, response fields, authentication, or rate limits change.
anti_patterns: Do not publish vague response descriptions such as returns data.
Do not include real API keys, tokens, passwords, or customer data in examples.
Do not document only success paths while omitting errors and limits.

faq.question: Can this skill create OpenAPI documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides OpenAPI 3.0 structure for paths, schemas, responses, authentication, and reusable components.

faq.question: Can it document GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is intended for GraphQL schemas as well as REST APIs and SDK methods.

faq.question: Does it run Swagger UI?
faq.answer: No. It shows setup guidance, but it does not host or deploy an interactive documentation site.

faq.question: Will it test API examples automatically?
faq.answer: No. Users should verify generated examples against a staging or test environment before publishing.

faq.question: Can it review existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify unclear descriptions, missing errors, weak examples, and incomplete authentication details.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with secrets?
faq.answer: Do not provide real secrets. Use placeholder tokens, sample passwords, and scrubbed customer data in all prompts.
